conjecture_title: Informal Conjecture (Induced Arithmetic Removal for Arbitrary Abelian Groups)
paper_title: Induced arithmetic removal: complexity 1 patterns over finite fields
summary: The conjecture asking for an induced arithmetic removal lemma for arbitrary complexity-1 patterns with integer coefficients over arbitrary abelian groups remains open. A 2024 paper by Gladkova (arXiv:2412.15170) extends the Fox–Tidor–Zhao result by achieving complete removal for partition-regular complexity-1 patterns, but stays within the finite-field setting and does not address the arbitrary abelian group case. No work resolving the conjecture over general abelian groups was found in the indexed literature.

since_posted.title: Induced arithmetic removal for partition-regular patterns of complexity 1
since_posted.claim: Proves complete induced arithmetic removal for partition-regular complexity-1 patterns over finite fields, extending the Fox–Tidor–Zhao framework, but does not address the extension to arbitrary abelian groups.

search_queries: Fox Tidor Zhao "induced arithmetic removal" abelian groups conjecture
arxiv 1911.03427 induced arithmetic removal complexity finite fields citations
notes: The conjecture is deliberately left informal in the source paper due to unresolved issues with infinite-complexity patterns and trivial solutions. The 2024 Gladkova paper is the most directly related follow-up work but remains restricted to finite fields. No resolution over arbitrary abelian groups was found.
